reviews.author: David Sterba
reviews.summary: reviewer pointed out that the GET_CSUMS ioctl is used as a hint in initial filtering, but final deduplication uses full byte comparison via memcmp(), suggesting this might not be sufficient for collision detection
reviews.raw_body:
It's used as a hint in the initial filter (e.g. in
https://github.com/lakshmipathi/dduper), the final deduplication does
full byte compariison (memcmp() inside vfs_dedupe_file_range_compare()).

reviews.author: Mark Harmstone (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the 'insane mode' using SHA256 should be safe, without committing to further verification or testing.
reviews.raw_body:
I've only had a brief look at the project, but what Lakshmipathi calls 
the "insane mode" ought to be safe for SHA256.

2026-04-15.patch_summary: This patch adds a new ioctl (input/output control) called BTRFS_IOC_GET_CSUMS to the btrfs file system, which allows userspace applications to retrieve checksums for files without having to manually calculate them. This is done to improve performance and reduce the load on the system, as calculating checksums manually can be time-consuming. The patch introduces a new interface that will allow the kernel to efficiently provide this information.
